college.institution_type: Indian Institute of Technology; autonomous statutory institution
college.address: Indian Institute of Technology Delhi, Hauz Khas, New Delhi 110016, India

additional_campuses.name: IIT Delhi Abu Dhabi
additional_campuses.reason: Separate campus must not be mixed with New Delhi campus decision data.

accreditation.item: Autonomous statutory organisation under the Institutes of Technology Act

admission_routes.qualifying_exam: JEE Main qualification followed by JEE Advanced, under current official rules
admission_routes.admission_process: Qualify JEE Advanced, register and fill choices in JoSAA, receive seat allocation, complete reporting and institute admission formalities.
admission_routes.board_eligibility: As prescribed in the official JEE Advanced 2026 information brochure; manual final check required.
admission_routes.category_reservation: OPEN, GEN-EWS, OBC-NCL, SC, ST and PwD pools as shown in JoSAA seat matrix.
admission_routes.foreign_national_route: Separate JEE Advanced/JoSAA rules may apply to foreign candidates; verify current brochure.
admission_routes.branch_change_rule: Permitted after first year within New Delhi campus subject to >8 CGPA for General/EWS/OBC, >7 for SC/ST/PwD, completion of first-year credits and no disciplinary action; cross-campus change is not permitted.

hostels_and_facilities.counselling_centre: Student counselling and welfare support are available; exact current service directory should be verified.
hostels_and_facilities.transport: Walkable residential campus with e-rickshaw/manual rickshaw services reported around visitor parking; Delhi Metro access.
hostels_and_facilities.accessibility: Office of Accessible Education and NIRF-reported ramps/lifts, walking aids and accessible toilets across most buildings.
hostels_and_facilities.security: Institutional security and hostel access rules; detailed current procedures require verification.
hostels_and_facilities.hostel_rules: Alcohol and narcotics prohibited; allotment, visitor and residence rules are administered by BHM.
hostels_and_facilities.anti_ragging: Institutional anti-ragging framework expected; current helpline and undertaking links require manual verification.
hostels_and_facilities.grievance_mechanism: BHM, Dean of Students, Board for Student Welfare and institute grievance channels.

location.official_address: Indian Institute of Technology Delhi, Hauz Khas, New Delhi 110016, India
location.nearest_metro: IIT Delhi Metro Station (Magenta Line); Hauz Khas interchange is approximately 2.5 km away
location.nearest_bus_terminal: IIT Gate / Hauz Khas DTC stops; major ISBT approximately 21 km
location.technology_ecosystem: Central South Delhi location with access to Delhi-NCR research, consulting, startup and corporate ecosystems including Gurugram and Noida.
location.cost_of_living_band: High for off-campus Delhi-NCR housing; institute accommodation is separately charged.
location.data_origin: Official address and transport directions; coordinates, hospital distance, travel time and city-centre distance are map-derived approximations.

senior_junior_culture.summary: Official student boards and hostel governance create structured peer interaction, while public evidence about batch hierarchy or mentorship quality is limited.
positive_themes: Peer networks and hostel friendships are repeatedly mentioned
negative_themes: Insufficient recent independent evidence on club hierarchy or senior-junior power dynamics

academic_culture.summary: The credit system offers curricular flexibility, projects and minors, but survey and first-person evidence indicate that workload and balancing academics with activities can be difficult.
positive_themes: Revised curriculum emphasises flexibility, hands-on learning and projects
First-year student account reports approachable faculty and two projects
negative_themes: 70–80% struggle with academic/extracurricular balance in the reported fresher survey
Academic pressure is a meaningful decision factor

hostel_reality.summary: Hostel life is central and student-managed, but capacity constraints mean sharing is common and conditions vary by hostel. Public reports on mess, water and cleanliness conflict and should not be generalised.
positive_themes: Strong hostel friendships reported by many survey respondents
Multiple campus food outlets and laundry facilities
negative_themes: In-campus capacity is constrained across total enrolment
Mixed reports on rooms, mess, water and washrooms
Exact 2026-27 BHM charges require manual confirmation

club_and_campus_life.summary: IIT Delhi has a broad cultural, technical, publication, welfare, sports and entrepreneurship ecosystem. Current activity is clearly visible at board level, but the latest activity of every individual club was not independently verified.
negative_themes: Club selection fairness and first-year access are not documented consistently

coding_and_placement_culture.summary: Official placement outcomes and recruiter participation indicate a very strong employment ecosystem, while independent student-level evidence on placement-cell transparency and non-CSE software access is insufficient.
positive_themes: Large number of offers and PPOs
Major technology, finance and consulting recruiters
negative_themes: Company-wise roles, packages and branch eligibility are not publicly complete
Placement-office snapshots use different denominators from NIRF
coding_and_placement_culture.evidence_strength: Strong official signal; limited student-reported signal

locality_and_student_life.summary: The Hauz Khas campus is unusually well connected for a residential institute, with its own metro station and access to South Delhi and NCR opportunities. Off-campus living costs and Delhi air pollution are potential concerns.
positive_themes: Proximity to AIIMS and central South Delhi
Access to Delhi-NCR internships and events
negative_themes: Traffic and seasonal air-quality concerns require personal consideration

overall_satisfaction.summary: Evidence supports exceptional academic, research, placement and extracurricular opportunity, alongside significant workload and heterogeneous hostel experiences. It is best suited to students comfortable with a demanding, highly active environment.
negative_themes: Some official operating details change by semester

parent_mode_summary.summary: IIT Delhi offers highly verified academic and placement strength, all-India JEE Advanced admission, category-linked tuition relief and excellent Delhi connectivity. Parent-side checks should focus on exact 2026-27 BHM/mess charges, hostel allotment and room sharing, final placement denominators, scholarship renewal documentation and the student’s comfort with a high-pressure metropolitan environment.

student_mode_summary.summary: The New Delhi campus offers 19 UG programme options, branch-change possibility after first year, strong technical/cultural clubs, major recruiter access and metropolitan exposure. Students should select branch on curriculum fit rather than assuming branch change, and should expect demanding coursework plus hostel conditions that differ across buildings.

decision_risks.risk: Choosing a branch mainly on the expectation of branch change
decision_risks.evidence: Branch change is conditional, capacity-constrained and never guaranteed.
Published institute fees exclude separate BHM and mess charges.

decision_risks.risk: Treating placement-office offer counts as placement percentage
decision_risks.evidence: OCS snapshots and NIRF use different scopes and denominators.

decision_risks.risk: Assuming all hostels have uniform facilities and mess quality
decision_risks.evidence: Official capacity and governance differ; student reports are hostel-specific and conflicting.

decision_risks.risk: Academic workload conflicts with expectations of a relaxed campus experience
decision_risks.evidence: Survey-based and first-person sources report balance challenges despite extensive opportunities.

decision_risks.risk: Off-campus Delhi costs and seasonal air quality
decision_risks.evidence: Metropolitan access is strong but housing and environmental conditions may matter personally.
